key_points: Democracy is a form of government where people elect their leaders
Democratic governments are accountable to citizens
Elections allow people to choose their representatives
Democracy encourages participation in decision making
examples: Citizens participating in protests or discussions
concepts: Accountability means government must answer to citizens
Legitimacy means people accept the authority of the government
Majority rule means decisions are taken according to the majority
exam_tips: Remember key democratic features like accountability and legitimacy
Use simple examples to explain democracy
Mention elections when discussing democratic processes
Write short and clear definitions in exams
